food_name: Popcorn, Buttered (Cinema Large, Machine-Popped, Artificial Butter Flavouring, Salted)
aliases: 牛油爆米花 (Niú Yóu Bào Mǐ Huā)

chinese: 牛油爆米花 (Niú Yóu Bào Mǐ Huā)

region_of_origin: United States (adopted as universal Singapore cinema snack)
food_subgroup: Machine-popped corn kernels coated with artificial butter flavouring (palm oil or coconut oil blend), salted

ontology.foodex2_description: Buttered popcorn — machine-popped, palm/coconut oil butter flavouring, salted (Singapore cinema large)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for cinema-style artificially buttered salted popcorn; A0CZC (popcorn) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for buttered cinema popcorn as a specific food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cinema bucket of buttered popcorn weighs approximately 100g of popped corn, representing a typical single-person serving at major Singapore cinemas such as Golden Village, Shaw, or Cathay.

nutrient_source.title: Popcorn, Buttered (Cinema) — USDA SR Legacy / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for buttered popcorn, adapted to reflect Singapore cinema-style machine-popped product with palm oil artificial butter flavouring and liberal salting. Sodium value (658mg/100g) reflects the higher salt load typical of commercial cinema popcorn versus standard USDA buttered popcorn entries. Saturated fat (14g/100g) reflects palm oil coating rather than dairy butter.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Popcorn — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: Popcorn GI of approximately 55–65 (medium) is well-established in the University of Sydney GI Database. A GI of 62 is used as the central estimate for machine-popped buttered salted popcorn. The intact whole-grain structure of popped corn and its high dietary fibre content (9.5g/100g) moderate the GI relative to other corn-based snacks. GL per 100g serving = (62 × 43g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 26.6, classified as high GL per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content at 658mg per 100g serving
hypertension.guidance: A single 100g large cinema serving delivers 658mg sodium — approximately one-third of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000mg — making this food unsuitable for patients with hypertension; unsalted homemade air-popped popcorn (approximately 4mg sodium/100g) is a strongly preferred alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (62) with high glycemic load per serving (GL 26.6) due to large carbohydrate quantity (52.5g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although popcorn has a moderate GI of 62 — lower than expected for corn due to its intact grain structure and high fibre (9.5g/100g) — a full 100g cinema serving carries a glycemic load of 26.6, which is clinically significant; patients with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to 30–40g and avoid the added fat and salt of cinema-style preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (14g/100g from palm oil) and trans fat (0.2g/100g) combined with very high sodium (658mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cinema buttered popcorn is high in palmitic-acid-rich saturated fat from palm oil (14g/100g), contains trace trans fat (0.2g/100g from oil degradation), and delivers very high sodium (658mg/100g), all of which are established cardiovascular risk factors; patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product and choose unsalted, oil-free air-popped popcorn instead.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat from palm oil (14g/100g) with documented pro-inflammatory palmitic acid content
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Palm oil used in cinema popcorn butter flavouring is rich in palmitic acid, a long-chain saturated fatty acid associated with NF-κB inflammatory pathway activation; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit consumption and prefer air-popped popcorn with no added oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cinema buttered popcorn is Singapore's quintessential movie-going snack — the large bucket of fluffy, golden, artificially buttered popcorn is as essential to the cinema experience as the film itself. Major Singapore cinemas (Golden Village, Shaw, Cathay, Filmgarde) sell popcorn in medium and large buckets, typically pre-salted and coated in an artificial butter flavouring that is almost always a palm or coconut oil blend with diacetyl-free butter flavour concentrate, rarely containing real dairy butter. The large bucket contains approximately 100g of popped corn — a deceptively large physical volume for the weight due to the low density of puffed corn kernels. Nutritionally, cinema popcorn is a calorie-dense and sodium-heavy snack: 477 kcal per 100g, 658mg sodium, and 28.5g total fat (14g saturated fat from palm oil coating). The high saturated fat content from palm oil is a key concern for cardiovascular and inflammatory conditions. Despite its whole-grain origin, the glycemic load per 100g serving is 26.6 (GI 62, medium), which is clinically significant for patients managing type 2 diabetes. The food is naturally dairy-free, gluten-free, vegan, and halal, making it accessible across Singapore's diverse population. Popcorn is colloquially known as 牛油爆米花 (niú yóu bào mǐ huā) in Chinese, Popcorn Mentega in Malay, and வெண்ணெய் பாப்கார்ன் in Tamil. It is eaten mindlessly during 2-hour films, making portion awareness particularly important. The taste is a balance of salty and artificial buttery notes, with a crunchy yet tender texture. The aroma is distinctively sweet and savory, often permeating cinema lobbies.
common_user_queries: is cinema popcorn bad for you

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for buttered popcorn, adapted for Singapore cinema-style palm oil artificial butter flavouring and commercial salting levels. GI value of 62 is well-supported by the University of Sydney GI Database for popcorn. Ontology anchors are defensible. All mandatory multilingual names for Singapore cuisine context are present. Aliases are fully consistent between flat array and structured aliases_by_language object.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for palm-oil-coated cinema popcorn is not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026; total fat and saturated fat are confirmed. water_g set to -1: moisture content for machine-popped cinema popcorn not reported in the primary source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — this product uses palm oil (plant-based) artificial butter flavouring with no dairy butter, so cholesterol is naturally zero per product formulation. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this food type. trans_fat_g of 0.2g/100g reflects minor oil degradation during commercial popping, consistent with USDA data for commercially popped popcorn. Sodium value of 658mg/100g reflects the higher salt load of Singapore cinema-style popcorn versus standard USDA buttered popcorn entries; this adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. GL per serving calculated as (GI 62 × available carbohydrate 43g) / 100 = 26.6 for a 100g serving.
